question: When a feature is in a non-terminal phase but its status is 'gate_blocked' or 'recirculated', which column should the card appear in?
options: The column matching its current_phase (e.g. a recirculated planning feature stays in the Planning column)
A separate 'Blocked' or 'Recirculated' column appended after Delivery
The column of the phase it was recirculated back to (target_phase)
Other

question: Should the Kanban board be the default view on the Dashboard, or should the current list view remain default?
options: List view stays default; Kanban is opt-in via toggle
Kanban becomes default; list view is opt-in via toggle
Remember the user's last choice (persist in localStorage)
Other

question: What card information should be visible on a Kanban card (beyond title)?
options: Title + priority + status badge (minimal, matching current FeatureCard)
Title + priority + status badge + pending questions indicator
Title + priority + status badge + pending questions indicator + last updated timestamp
Title + priority + status badge + pending questions indicator + last updated + gate result indicator
Other

question: Should empty columns (phases with no features) be shown or hidden?
options: Always show all six phase columns, even if empty (consistent board layout)
Hide columns that have zero features (compact board)
Other

question: Should users be able to drag-and-drop cards between columns to change a feature's phase, or is the board read-only?
options: Read-only board (cards move only via the pipeline; clicking navigates to detail)
Drag-and-drop to advance phase only (forward moves allowed, no skips)
Full drag-and-drop to any column (admin override of pipeline ordering)
Other

question: How should the board handle a large number of features in a single column (e.g. 50 features in Construction)?
options: Scroll within each column independently (CSS overflow-y on column body)
Show all cards, column grows with page scroll (simple, no pagination)
Show first 10 cards with a 'show N more' expander inside the column
Other

question: On narrow viewports (mobile / small tablet), how should the six-column board behave?
options: Horizontal scroll (board stays 6 columns wide, viewport scrolls sideways)
Stack columns vertically (each phase becomes a collapsible section)
Show first 3 columns, horizontally scroll to reveal the rest
Other
